componentAlignment.explanation: The proposal bypasses the parent contract as the first dependency.
It reverses the required sequencing by implementing local fixtures before shared compatibility is pinned.
Feedback is deferred until after local fixtures pass, so failures cannot shape the contract early.

dependencyViolations.explanation: The proposal implements calibration before consuming the contract.

evidenceGroundingGaps.missingEvidence: No evidence that child issues share the same compatibility target before fixture work.

nearNeighborResistance.explanation: The proposal is the calibration-first near-neighbor that the model rejects.
Inferring a local shape risks duplicate contract semantics.

fLabelHint.label: F3
fLabelHint.explanation: Real process rationale is load-bearing but violates the required sequence and target ownership.
